data.title: Ridley Posts Lower Fiscal H1 Earnings Despite Higher Revenue; Shares Fall 6%
data.description:
Ridley reported Thursday that its earnings per diluted share in the fiscal first half fell to AU$0.065 from AU$0.066 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha were expecting EPS of AU$0.075.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$658.9 million, up from AU$637.8 million in the same period a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$665.7 million.

The provider of animal nutrition solutions expects its growth plan to deliver AU$5 million per annum in cost savings from fiscal 2026.

The board declared an interim dividend of AU$0.0475 per share, up from the AU$0.044 in the previously reported period, payable on April 24 to shareholders on record as of April 4.

Shares of RIC fell almost 6% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: South Korean shares drop as traders gauge Trump's latest tariff threat
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI falls, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won weakens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares fell on Thursday, poised to snap a seven-day rally, pressured by chip, automakers and biopharmaceutical stocks as traders kept to the sidelines following U.S. President Donald Trump's latest tariff threat.

** The benchmark KOSPI was down 16.78 points, or 0.63%, at 2,654.74, as of 0059 GMT. The index closed on Wednesday at its highest point since late September 2024.

** Trump said he will announce tariffs related to lumber, cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals "over the next month or sooner".

** Shares of chipmaker Samsung Electronics fell 0.34% and peer SK Hynix lost 0.92%, while Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp traded down 0.98% and 1.26%, respectively.

** Samsung Biologics fell 1.73% while biopharmaceutical peer Celltrion shed 0.17%.

** Among other index heavyweights, battery manufacturers and steel producers rose, while e-commerce firms were mixed.

** South Korean stocks with exposure to businesses in China rallied on media reports that Beijing plans to lift an unofficial ban on Korean contents in the first half of 2025.

** Of the total 937 traded issues, 430 shares advanced, while 446 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 169.9 billion won ($118.20 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,442.1 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.05% lower than its previous close at 1,441.4.

** In money and debt markets, March futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) fell 0.02 point to 106.61.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 1.6 basis points to 2.639%, while the benchmark 10-year yield climbed 1.9 basis points to 2.888%.

($1 = 1,437.4500 won)

data.title: Hang Seng Tech Index Opens 1.1% Lower at 5610.81
Day 31 in Trump World: a Roundup — WSJ
data.description:
By Tali Arbel and Victoria Albert

President Trump is moving at lightning speed in his second term to execute his MAGA agenda. Here is a look at his notable actions and statements on Wednesday, Feb. 19. This list will be updated throughout the day.

  • Trump took a short flight from Palm Beach International Airport near Mar-a-Lago to the Miami airport, landing around 9 a.m. ET. He then went to the Trump National Doral Miami hotel.
  • Trump championed the House of Representatives' budget bill with deep spending cuts over a narrower approach in the Senate focused on border and defense spending. He said the House approach was better because it "implements my FULL American First Agenda, EVERYTHING, not just parts of it!"
  • Trump escalated his criticisms of Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky as his administration lays the groundwork for talks with Russia to end the war. On Truth Social, Trump called the Ukrainian leader a "Dictator without Elections" and said he "better move fast or he is not going to have a Country left." Zelensky had said Trump was repeating Russian propaganda points in claiming that Ukraine started the war and that Zelensky has lost Ukrainians' support.
  • Trump celebrated the U.S. Transportation Department's move to rescind approval of New York City's congestion-pricing program, which began last month. He wrote on Truth Social: "CONGESTION PRICING IS DEAD. Manhattan, and all of New York, is SAVED. LONG LIVE THE KING!" The Metropolitan Transportation Authority sued over the reversal and said congestion pricing would continue unless a court ordered the agency to stop it.
  • Trump spoke at the FII Priority Summit, a Saudi-backed investment conference in Miami Beach. He discussed several of his administration's efforts, including DOGE and a proposal to potentially use a portion of the department's savings to provide direct payments to Americans. Read more about the idea.
  • Trump left the conference and arrived at the Miami airport. He is expected to return to Washington, D.C., tonight, and sign executive orders aboard Air Force One.

Catch up: What Trump said and did on Day 30.| Trump's most significant actions so far.

Write to Tali Arbel at tali.arbel@wsj.com and Victoria Albert at victoria.albert@wsj.com

data.title: TFI International Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• TFI International Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.19​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, lower than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.71. The mean expectation of sixteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.60 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.50 to $1.70 per share.

  • Revenue rose 5.5% to $2.08 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $2.17 billion.

  • TFI International Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $1.03​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $83.88 million.

  • TFI International Inc shares had fallen by 6.9% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 3.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 12 "strong buy" or "buy," 6 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the ground freight & logistics peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for TFI International Inc is C$210.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 20 at 01:22 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Carvana says it had a record year. Why its stock is falling anyway.
data.description:
By Claudia Assis

Carvana's stock drops in extended trading after earnings

Carvana Co. may have spooked investors late Wednesday as the online used-car platform saw a drop in its revenue per vehicle sold retail.

Carvana (CVNA) reported fourth-quarter numbers that beat expectations, along with "record" 2024 net income of $404 million, but the stock fell by as much as 12% in the extended session Wednesday.

Carvana made good on its outlook for 2024: It reported earnings before interest and taxes of $1.378 billion; it had called for adjusted Ebitda "significantly above" the $1 billion to $1.2 billion range.

For all of 2025 and the first quarter, the company said it expects "significant growth" in adjusted Ebitda, but it didn't provide a range.

Ahead of the report, analysts at Davidson said it was likely that Carvana was seeing "accelerating growth" in the fourth quarter.

"That's the good news. The risk is that that is already in consensus estimates, and a rich valuation demands more beats and raises."

The stock has gained 13% in the past three months, well above the advance of about 4% for the S&P 500 index SPX. The stock has quintupled in the last 12 months, versus gains of 23% for the broader index.

Then there was Carvana's per-unit revenue for retail vehicles: it fell 4.5% to $22,312 in the quarter, from $23,354 in the fourth quarter of 2023.

Carvana said it sold 114,379 cars retail, as opposed to wholesale, in the fourth quarter, up 50% year on year.

The company reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of 74 cents on sales of $3.55 billion, which compare with FactSet expectations of 31 cents on sales of $3.34 billion for the quarter.

-Claudia Assis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Peloton Interactive Executives Sell Shares to Cover Tax Liabilities and Other Reasons
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Baig SaqibChief Accounting OfficerSell$445,985Form 4
Coddington Elizabeth FChief Financial OfficerSell$1,642,560Form 4
Cotter Jennifer CunninghamChief Content OfficerSell$1,256,508Form 4
Rendich Andrew SChief Supply Chain OfficerSell$1,163,129Form 4

Several top executives at Peloton Interactive have recently sold shares of the company, primarily to cover tax liabilities associated with the settlement of Restricted Stock Units (RSUs).

Saqib Baig, the Chief Accounting Officer, sold 46,078 Class A Common Stock shares on February 18 and 19, 2025, at weighted average prices of $9.6109 and $9.7492, respectively, for a total of $445,985. Following these sales, Baig directly owns 130,031 shares of Peloton Interactive.

Elizabeth F Coddington, the Chief Financial Officer, sold 170,819 shares on February 18, 2025, at weighted average prices of $9.6371 and $9.5673, totaling $1,642,560. Post-transaction, Coddington directly owns 275,212 shares.

Jennifer Cunningham Cotter, the Chief Content Officer, sold 131,617 shares at a weighted average price of $9.5467 per share on February 18, 2025, totaling $1,256,508. Cotter's direct ownership now stands at 334,964 shares.

Andrew S. Rendich, the Chief Supply Chain Officer, sold 121,883 shares at a weighted average price of $9.543 per share on February 18, 2025, for a total of $1,163,129. Following this sale, Rendich directly owns 404,188 shares of Peloton Interactive.

data.title: Australia's Megaport soars on profit beat, upbeat outlook
data.description:
** Shares of cloud connectivity provider Megaport rise 23% to A$11.740, their highest level since June 20, 2024

** Stock top pct gainer in the benchmark ASX 200 index , which is down 1% as of 0050 GMT

** MP1 rose as much as 27.4% to A$12.150 earlier, marking its biggest intraday pct jump since January 30, 2024

** More than 3 million shares change hands, vs the 30-day average of less than a million

** Megaport reports annual recurring revenue (ARR) of A$226.6 million ($143.9 million), up 18% from last year, and 4% ahead of Visible Alpha consensus

** Hikes lower end of its FY25 revenue outlook to between A$216 million and A$222 million, from prior range of A$214 million and A$222 million

** Citi says this is conservative as co's 1H25 ARR implies A$220 million revenue without any growth in 2H

** MP1 up 57% this year, including session's moves

($1 = 1.5748 Australian dollars)

data.title: Coronado Global Resources Swings to Loss in 2024, Reports Lower Revenue
data.description:
Coronado Global Resources reported Thursday that it swung to a loss of $0.65 per diluted share in fiscal 2024, compared with earnings of $0.93 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha expected a loss per diluted share of $0.67.

Total revenue for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$2.51

billion, down 13% compared with AU$2.89 billion a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected a total revenue of $2.51 billion.

The board declared a dividend of AU$0.005 per Chess depository interest, unchanged from AU$0.005 in the previously reported period, payable April 4 to shareholders on record as of March 12.
